items.scenario: Your code sets temperature = config.get("temperature") or 0.7, and a user explicitly passes temperature = 0.0 for deterministic output.
items.question: What goes wrong and how do you fix it?
items.answer: 0.0 is falsy, so `or` discards it and the model runs at 0.7 instead of deterministically. Replace the short-circuit default with an explicit None check: value = config.get("temperature"); temperature = value if value is not None else 0.7.

items.scenario: You compute batches = total_items / batch_size and later use range(batches), which raises a TypeError.
items.question: Why does it fail and what is the correct operator?
items.answer: / always yields a float, and range() needs an int. Use floor division: batches = total_items // batch_size (and consider -(-total_items // batch_size) or math.ceil to round up for a final partial batch).

items.scenario: A guard `text = resp.choices[0].message.content` crashes when the API returns no choices.
items.question: How can short-circuit evaluation make this safer?
items.answer: Test the value before indexing: text = resp.choices[0].message.content if resp and resp.choices else "". The `and` short-circuits, so the indexing only runs when resp and resp.choices are truthy, avoiding the IndexError/AttributeError.
